name: Return to Agramunt - Circular through the dry and irrigated fields
name_original: Vuelta hasta Agramunt - Circular por los campos de secano y regadío
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Tàrrega are characterized by a diverse landscape, ranging from the flat expanses of the Urgell Plain to areas with significant elevation gain. The region features agricultural fields, particularly barley and wheat, and waterways such as the Sió River and the Canal d'Urgell. Cyclists can explore routes alongside these canals and rivers, offering both relaxed rides and more demanding experiences. Best road cycling routes around Tàrrega The most popular road cycling route is Bordering the Urgell canal - Circular from Tàrrega, an easy 17.3 miles (27.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 23 minutes to complete. This route offers a gentle ride alongside the Canal d'Urgell. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Return to Agramunt - Circular through the dry and irrigated fields, a moderate 42.7 miles (68.8 km) path. This route traverses both dry and irrigated agricultural fields, providing varied scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Church of Sant Martí de Maldà – Conesa Medieval Walled Village loop from Tàrrega, a 67.6 miles (108.7 km) trail leading through the region's varied terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 26 minutes. Road cycling around Tàrrega is defined by the flat Urgell Plain, the Sió River, and the extensive Canal d'Urgell. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy canal-side rides to challenging routes with considerable elevation gain. The routes in Tàrrega are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. More than 800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Tàrrega's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Tàrrega?
A: There are over 60 road cycling routes available around Tàrrega, offering a wide range of options for different preferences and skill levels. These include 9 easy routes, 35 moderate routes, and 20 difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Tàrrega?
A: The terrain around Tàrrega is diverse, featuring both flat expanses and areas with significant elevation gain. You'll find routes traversing the agricultural fields of the Urgell Plain, as well as picturesque paths alongside waterways like the Sió River and the extensive Canal d'Urgell. This mix allows for both relaxed rides and more demanding experiences.

Q: Are there easy, flat road cycling routes suitable for a relaxed ride?
A: Yes, Tàrrega offers several easy, flat routes perfect for a relaxed ride. A popular choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939006604/bordering-the-urgell-canal-circular-from-tarrega" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bordering the Urgell canal - Circular from Tàrrega&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 27.8 km trail that provides a gentle ride alongside the Canal d'Urgell.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenge, Tàrrega has routes with considerable elevation gain. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40351509" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Sant Martí de Maldà – Conesa Medieval Walled Village loop from Tàrrega&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 108.7 km trail that leads through varied terrain and includes substantial climbing.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I visit while road cycling in Tàrrega?
A: The region around Tàrrega is rich in historical and cultural landmarks. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3309475" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de Vallbona&lt;/a&gt;, an 11th-century Cistercian abbey. Another notable point is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/348035" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilar de Almenara Tower&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views of the Urgell plain. The historic town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5072288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera&lt;/a&gt;, located on a hilltop, also provides historical charm and good vistas.

Q: Are there scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes offer scenic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/348035" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilar de Almenara Tower&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, providing magnificent panoramic views of the Urgell plain, and on clear days, even the Seu Vella de Lleida. Many routes traversing the agricultural fields also offer expansive, open vistas.

Q: Are there long-distance road cycling routes near Tàrrega?
A: Yes, Tàrrega offers options for longer rides. The Canal d'Urgell itself provides a 150 km route from the pre-Pyrenees to Montoliu, indicating opportunities for extended cycling alongside this waterway. For a specific route, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31480964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallbona de les Monges – Church of Sant Martí de Maldà loop from Tàrrega&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 86 km.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Tàrrega?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in Tàrrega are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939012355/return-to-agramunt-circular-through-the-dry-and-irrigated-fields" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to Agramunt - Circular through the dry and irrigated fields&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 68.8 km loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31756280" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Tàrrega – Church of Sant Martí de Maldà loop from Tàrrega&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 41.3 km route.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Tàrrega?
A: The road cycling routes in Tàrrega are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscape, which includes both the flat Urgell Plain and areas with elevation gain, as well as the scenic paths along the Sió River and Canal d'Urgell.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Tàrrega?
A: Spring and autumn are generally considered the best times for road cycling in Tàrrega. The weather during these seasons is typically mild and pleasant, making for comfortable riding conditions. Summers can be quite warm, especially in the open plains, while winters are cooler but often still suitable for cycling on clear days.

Q: Are there parking options available for cyclists in Tàrrega?
A: As a regional hub, Tàrrega offers various public parking facilities within the town. Cyclists can typically find parking in municipal lots or on-street parking areas, especially near the town center or residential zones, which can serve as convenient starting points for routes.

Q: Is Tàrrega suitable for family-friendly road cycling?
A: Yes, Tàrrega offers family-friendly road cycling options, particularly along the flatter sections of the Canal d'Urgell and the Sió River. These routes provide gentle gradients and scenic surroundings, making them suitable for cyclists of varying ages and abilities. There are 9 easy routes in total that families can explore.
tips: The municipality of Agramunt is located in the Urgell region, located at the foot of the Sió river. It is a city that is fortified and has a large number of small streets with narrow avenues.

Municipality and head of the region of La Segarra, in the center of the riverside of Cervera, extended on both sides of the river of Cervera or river of Ondara, on top of the structural platforms of La Segarra. The town can be seen from afar as it is located on top of a hill.

Municipality located in the south-west of the Segarra region that borders the Granyanella thermal areas, to the north; Cervera, to the northeast; Ribera d'Ondara, to the east; Montornès de Segarra, to the south, and Verdú and Tàrrega and El Talladell (both in the Urgell region), to the southwest.
Part of the municipal area is part of the Protected Natural Area of Granyena.
